req.title: Hr-Admin updates performance-review
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to update a performance-review through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: This test suite validates an hr-admin's ability to update performance-reviews via UI form with mandatory field validation, audit trail generation, and proper HTTP status codes. The suite covers the happy path (positive), authentication/authorization failures (negative), and boundary constraints on field lengths (boundary). Test design applies Equivalence Partitioning to group valid/invalid inputs, Boundary Value Analysis for field length limits, and Negative Path heuristics to exercise error conditions. All tests are deterministic, idempotent, and traceable to the requirement's expected outcomes and error pathways.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_updates_performance_review_successfully
actions: send PUT request to /performance-reviews/PR-001
payload contains: rating=4, reviewerComments='Good performance', reviewDate='2024-01-15'
include valid auth token in header
expected: audit record created with action='UPDATE' and hr-admin user id
tests.executable:
import requests
import json
from datetime import datetime

def test_hr_admin_updates_performance_review_successfully():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    headers = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <valid_hr_admin_token>'}
    review_id = 'PR-001'
    
    payload = {
        'rating': 4,
        'reviewerComments': 'Good performance',
        'reviewDate': '2024-01-15'
    }
    
    response = requests.put(
        f'{base_url}/performance-reviews/{review_id}',
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    
    assert response.status_code in [200, 201]
    data = response.json()
    assert data['id'] == review_id
    assert 'updated_at' in data
    assert data['rating'] == 4
    
    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f'{base_url}/audit?entity_id={review_id}&action=UPDATE',
        headers=headers
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    audits = audit_resp.json()
    assert len(audits) > 0
    assert audits[0]['user_role'] == 'hr-admin'

tests.name: test_hr_admin_update_missing_mandatory_field
actions: send PUT request to /performance-reviews/PR-002
payload omits mandatory field 'rating'
include valid auth token in header
expected: response includes error message referencing missing 'rating' field
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_hr_admin_update_missing_mandatory_field():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    headers = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <valid_hr_admin_token>'}
    review_id = 'PR-002'
    
    payload = {
        'reviewerComments': 'Good performance',
        'reviewDate': '2024-01-15'
    }
    
    response = requests.put(
        f'{base_url}/performance-reviews/{review_id}',
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    
    assert response.status_code == 400
    error_data = response.json()
    assert 'rating' in error_data.get('error', '').lower()
    
    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f'{base_url}/audit?entity_id={review_id}',
        headers=headers
    )
    initial_count = len(audit_resp.json())
    
    response2 = requests.put(
        f'{base_url}/performance-reviews/{review_id}',
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    audit_resp2 = requests.get(
        f'{base_url}/audit?entity_id={review_id}',
        headers=headers
    )
    assert len(audit_resp2.json()) == initial_count

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_performance_review
actions: send PUT request to /performance-reviews/PR-003
omit Authorization header
include valid payload
expected: response includes error message about authentication
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_performance_review():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    review_id = 'PR-003'
    
    payload = {
        'rating': 3,
        'reviewerComments': 'Needs improvement',
        'reviewDate': '2024-01-15'
    }
    
    response = requests.put(
        f'{base_url}/performance-reviews/{review_id}',
        json=payload
    )
    
    assert response.status_code == 401
    error_data = response.json()
    assert 'unauthorized' in error_data.get('error', '').lower() or \
           'authentication' in error_data.get('error', '').lower()

tests.name: test_non_hr_admin_role_cannot_update_performance_review
actions: send PUT request to /performance-reviews/PR-004
include valid employee auth token
include valid payload
expected: response includes error message about forbidden role
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_non_hr_admin_role_cannot_update_performance_review():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    headers = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <valid_employee_token>'}
    review_id = 'PR-004'
    
    payload = {
        'rating': 3,
        'reviewerComments': 'Needs improvement',
        'reviewDate': '2024-01-15'
    }
    
    response = requests.put(
        f'{base_url}/performance-reviews/{review_id}',
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    
    assert response.status_code == 403
    error_data = response.json()
    assert 'forbidden' in error_data.get('error', '').lower() or \
           'permission' in error_data.get('error', '').lower()

tests.name: test_reviewer_comments_field_boundary_max_length
preconditions: reviewerComments field has max length constraint (e.g. 5000 chars)
actions: send PUT request to /performance-reviews/PR-005
payload contains reviewerComments with exactly 5000 characters
other mandatory fields present and valid
expected: performance-review updated with full comment text
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_reviewer_comments_field_boundary_max_length():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    headers = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <valid_hr_admin_token>'}
    review_id = 'PR-005'
    max_comment = 'x' * 5000
    
    payload = {
        'rating': 4,
        'reviewerComments': max_comment,
        'reviewDate': '2024-01-15'
    }
    
    response = requests.put(
        f'{base_url}/performance-reviews/{review_id}',
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    
    assert response.status_code in [200, 201]
    data = response.json()
    assert data['reviewerComments'] == max_comment
    assert data['id'] == review_id

tests.name: test_reviewer_comments_field_exceeds_max_length
preconditions: reviewerComments field has max length constraint (e.g. 5000 chars)
actions: send PUT request to /performance-reviews/PR-006
payload contains reviewerComments exceeding 5000 characters
other mandatory fields present and valid
expected: response includes validation error for field length
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_reviewer_comments_field_exceeds_max_length():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    headers = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <valid_hr_admin_token>'}
    review_id = 'PR-006'
    over_limit = 'x' * 5001
    
    payload = {
        'rating': 4,
        'reviewerComments': over_limit,
        'reviewDate': '2024-01-15'
    }
    
    response = requests.put(
        f'{base_url}/performance-reviews/{review_id}',
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    
    assert response.status_code == 400
    error_data = response.json()
    assert 'length' in error_data.get('error', '').lower() or \
           'max' in error_data.get('error', '').lower()

tests.name: test_rating_field_boundary_valid_range
actions: send PUT request to /performance-reviews/PR-007 with rating=1
send PUT request to /performance-reviews/PR-007 with rating=5
include valid auth token and other mandatory fields
expected: audit records created for each update

tests.name: test_rating_field_outside_valid_range
actions: send PUT request to /performance-reviews/PR-008 with rating=0
send PUT request to /performance-reviews/PR-008 with rating=6
include valid auth token and other mandatory fields
expected: response includes validation error for invalid rating
